# Project plan

## Start-up phase

The project begins immediately after contract execution. In the first two weeks, I will set up a dedicated development branch on the existing `redlist` GitHub repository, and define the public API for the three modules. The MIT licence already applies to the package. Progress will be reported via posts on the R Consortium blog.

## Technical delivery

The project runs over 4 months. Target dates below assume a contract start date at the beginning of the funded period.

**Month 1 : Taxonomic name resolution (Milestone 1)**

Deliver functions with full unit tests and documentation. This includes handling of edge cases: species with no GBIF match, species with multiple equally ranked matches, and species where the IUCN name is itself a synonym in GBIF. The function will be tested against a set of 50 species spanning plants, mammals, birds, and insects to verify performance across taxonomic groups.

**Month 2: Occurrence retrieval (Milestone 2)**

Deliver function with deduplication logic, and documentation. This milestone includes a worked vignette showing the full retrieval workflow for two real species.

**Month 3 to 4: Assessment-readiness checks and metric calculation (Milestone 3)**

Deliver functions with all five checks, AOO and EOO metrics calculation.

## Other aspects

All code is developed openly on GitHub under the MIT licence.

## Budget and funding plan

The project is a labour-only budget at USD 50 per hour, covering 132 hours of part-time development over four months.

| Milestone | Work | Hours | Amount |
|------------------|------------------|------------------|------------------|
| Taxonomic resolution | `rl_gbif_resolve()`, tests, docs | 42 | USD 2,100 |
| Occurrence retrieval | `rl_occurrences()`, deduplication, vignette | 35 | USD 1,750 |
| Assessment | `rl_check_occurrences()`, `rl_aoo()`, `rl_eoo()`, integration, docs | 55 | USD 2,750 |
| **Total** |  |  132 | **USD 6,600** |
